observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Concerned offices to submit to the Accounting Office the distribution lists with pertinent distribution lists on the distributed supplies and materials from January to December 2023.
b. RSPO to (i) prepare and submit to Municipal Accountant the RSPI on the issued semi-expendable properties totaling ₱3.306 million for preparation of journal entry; and (ii) keep an updated ICS, stock cards and semi-expendable property cards for proper monitoring of receipt, issuance and accountability;
c. Municipal Accountant to (i) effect the proper adjustments on the unrecorded distribution of goods in the amount of ₱69,700.00 and unissued inventory items of already expended in the amount of ₱.200 million, and thereafter, adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the Inventories, and (ii) keep an updated supplies ledger card and semi-expendable property ledger cards on details of recorded Inventories; and
d. Municipal Inventory Team conduct physical inventory of all its inventory items and prepare and submit the RPCI and RPCSP not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Inventory Committee should (i) reconcile the balances of the inventory per books with the inventory reports, (ii) verify and analyze the discrepancies, and (iii) prepare adjustments to the affected records, if any, in compliance with the procedural guidelines on the receipt and issuance of inventory items prescribed in Chapter 7 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2022-004.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant take-up the appropriate adjusting entries for the (i) book reconciling items of ₱25,075.75; (ii) stale checks of ₱5,740.64, and (iii) unreleased checks of ₱1.607 million; and prepare and submit to the Audit Team the monthly BRS on time deposit accounts; and
b. Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant with Schedule of Unreleased Checks as of year-end for the proper restoration to CIB account to reflect the accurate cash balance in the financial statement.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for RPT and SET at the beginning of every year, to serve as basis in proper setting up of RPT/SET Receivables; and a monthly report on advance payments of RPT and SET for the forthcoming year, to facilitate the proper recording of Deferred Credits for advance payment; and
b. Municipal Accountant to set up Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable and Deferred Real Property Tax Income/Deferred Special Education Tax Income Receivables as of December 31, 2024, and every beginning of the succeeding years, based on the duly certified list provided by the Municipal Treasurer; and record the advance payment, discounts, fines and penalties on RPT and SET, in accordance with the COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015.

We recommended that the:
a. RSPO prepare and submit to the Municipal Accountant the RSPI on the issued semi-expendable properties totaling ₱3.862 million for preparation of journal entry;
b. Municipal Accountant (i) effect the proper adjustments on the unrecorded distribution of goods in the amount of ₱.385 million and unissued inventory items already expended in the amount of ₱.348 million, and thereafter, adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording the Inventories; and
c. Municipal Inventory Team prepare and submit a complete and detailed RPCI, in a manner prescribed in the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, taking into account the deficiencies noted in audit. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Inventory Committee should (i) reconcile the balances of the inventory per books with the inventory reports, (ii) verify and analyze the discrepancies, and (iii) prepare adjustments to the affected records, if any, in compliance with the procedural guidelines on the receipt and issuance of inventory items prescribed in Chapter 7 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and COA Circular No. 2022-004.

We recommended that the Management consider availing the one-time cleansing of accounts prescribed in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, to facilitate the reporting of reliable PPE account balance in the financial statements. We further recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) effect the necessary adjustments for the noted errors and omissions, particularly the non-provision of depreciation expense for five completed projects; unrecorded receipt of 18 PPE items and misclassified accounts amounting to ₱6.949 million; and (ii) maintain a separate SL for road networks and its component pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23, 2015;
b. The MENRO and concerned recipients of the unrecorded PPE items promptly submit to the Municipal Accountant and RSPO the IRP or equivalent Property Transfer Report duly supported with Deed of Donations, MOA, and other applicable documents for proper recording in the property cards and books of accounts; and
c. Property Inventory Team (i) conduct the physical inventory of all its property, plant and equipment, including Road Networks; (ii) prepare and submit the RPCPPE in accordance with the provisions in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 and the Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network System, not later than January 31 of the ensuing year; and (iii) reconcile property records with accounting ledgers pursuant to the guidelines prescribed in the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I. Further, the Property Unit shall observe the proper preparation and maintenance of PCs in accordance with the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Effect the appropriate adjusting entries to correct the noted errors in accounting for Loans Payable and other affected accounts; and
b. Perform periodic reconciliation of loan records with the lending banks to facilitate timely adjustment of any discrepancies, as warranted, and to ensure the accuracy of Loans Payable – Domestic balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the details of the outstanding balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and effect the necessary adjustments in compliance with the proper classification of accounts under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015, to reflect the correct balances of affected accounts and facilitate elimination of reciprocal accounts pursuant to IPSAS No. 6.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct a detailed review of the relevant information on the cited accounts/items, and include the necessary disclosures thereon in the revised NFS, in compliance with IPSAS and COA Circular Nos. 2015-008 and 2014-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Project Management and Implementation Unit (MPMIU) Engineer and the Municipal Accountant submit the lacking supporting documents for evaluation by the Audit Team. Furthermore, ensure that subsequent claims for infrastructure projects with variation orders, including time extensions, are supported with proper and complete documentation in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer, and Municipal Treasurer:
a) Analyze the composition of CIB balance under TF DBP-LP and revert back to GF the unutilized amount therefrom, after considering the necessary cash reserve for DBP loan repayments; and
b) Limit the transfer of funds from 20%DF to TF-DBP-LP to the actual amount of loan repayments to ensure the propriety and accuracy of recorded obligations and balance of allotment and prevent recurrence of unutilized funds.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that Management formulate uniform policies and procedures on the recruitment and management of COS/JO personnel for incorporation in the Human Resource Management Manual, consistent with the NGICS, RPGIAM, and Section 76 of the LGC. Additionally, we recommended considering the inclusion of policies on the following matters: (a) jobs or functions which may be directly outsourced through contract of service/ job order, (b) mandatory conditions in contracts of service and job orders pursuant to CSC regulations, (c) hiring procedures, (d) schedules and monitoring of attendance, absences, tardiness, and undertime, (f) mode, frequency, and timing of payment of wages, (g) documents needed to support claims for salaries, and (h) other benefits or entitlements, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit justification for the payments made despite the lacking CENRR required under the COA Circular No. 2017-001 dated June 19, 2017, for further evaluation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the appropriate expense accounts to record payments for services of JO personnel in the books of accounts, ensuring accurate and comparable balances of expense accounts in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the MDRRMO and MSWDO:
a. Provide a certified list of distributed LDRRMF welfare goods indicating the recipients and purpose/reason for distribution for further evaluation, and henceforth limit its distribution to persons adversely affected by calamities or disaster or other valid purposes; and
b. Maintain updated stock cards and inventory reports in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-002, and ensure that all distribution of welfare goods are properly documented using the RIS and the distribution lists with complete details as to purpose and the justification on reallocation to other programs, if necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Co-Chairperson of the Local School Board, promptly furnish the Municipal Budget Officer, the Sangguniang Bayan and the Regional Offices of DBM and DILG with a copy of the SEF Budget or the SEF Utilization Report for CYs 2024 and 2025, as necessary; and update the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports in at least three (3) conspicuous places pursuant to DepEd – DBM – DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, series of 2017, to ensure the transparency and accountability in the allocation and utilization of the SEF.

We recommended that the Municipal Health Officer submit written justification on the noted inconsistencies in the procurement of medicines amounting to ₱.932 million for further evaluation; and henceforth strictly implement the mandated ceiling prices in the procurement of medicines based on DPRI in compliance with DOH AO No. 2015-0051 dated December 17, 2015.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in coordination with the Municipal GAD Focal Point System, continue their efforts in mainstreaming GAD in the local policy-making, planning, programming and budgeting; monitor the effective implementation of GPB to prevent recurrence of unimplemented GAD PAPs; and whenever necessary, adopt remedial measures to ensure the execution of entire GAD PAPs and facilitate the attainment of gender-responsive governance.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the appropriate adjusting entries to correct the noted errors in accounting for Loans Payable; and henceforth perform periodic reconciliation of loan records with the lending banks and identify and adjust any discrepancies, as warranted, to ensure the accuracy of Loans Payable balance in the financial reports.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:

a. The Municipal Accountant (i) prepare and submit the BRS for  all  of  its
  bank accounts within the timeframe set forth in COA Circular No. 96- 011;
  (ii) effect the appropriate adjustments for the  book  reconciling  items
  amounting to  ₱11,293.37;  and  (iii)  take-up  the  necessary  adjusting
  entries to revert the total stale checks amounting to ₱48,930.87; and

b. Municipal Treasurer regularly coordinate with  and/or  request  from  the
  AGDB  the  monthly  bank  statements  or  copy  of  snapshots   of   cash
  transactions to facilitate timely reconciliation of CIB-LCCA, and observe
  the procedural guidelines outlined in Section 59 of  the  Manual  on  the
  NGAS for LGUs, Volume I regarding the  cancellation  and  replacement  of
  stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


a. concerned offices to submit to the Accounting Office  the  pertinent  RIS
  and/or  distribution lists on the issuance of the subject welfare  goods,
  foods supplies and other supplies and materials  inventory  amounting  to
  ₱542,575.00, to facilitate the proper recording in the books of accounts;



b. Municipal Accountant to take up  the  appropriate  adjusting  entries  to
  reclassify   the  items  amounting   to   ₱275,120.00   that   meet   the
  capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00[1] to  the  appropriate  Furniture
  and Fixtures account;

c.  On  unreliable  balance  of  Agricultural  and   Marine   Supplies   for
  Distribution and Agricultural  Produce  for  Distribution  accounts,  the
  Municipal Accountant in coordination with  the  Municipal  Agriculturist,
  exhaust all effort  to  trace  or  retrieve  the  records  and  documents
  onprocurement and issuances for the prior year’s balances  of  ₱83,412.00
  and ₱499,500.00, for proper recording  or  adjustment  in  the  books  of
  accounts, if warranted; and

d. Municipal Inventory Team conduct physical inventory of all its  inventory
  items and prepare and submit the RPCI not later than July 31 and  January
  31 of each  year  for  the  first  and  second  semesters,  respectively.
  Thereafter,  the  Municipal  Accountant  and  the   Municipal   Inventory
  Committee should (i) reconcile the balances of the  inventory  per  books
  with the inventory reports, (ii) verify and  analyze  the  discrepancies,
  and (iii) prepare  adjustments  to  the  affected  records,  if  any,  in
  compliance with the procedural guidelines on the receipt and issuance  of
  inventory items prescribed in Chapter 7 of Manual on  the  NGAS  for  LGUs,
  Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:


     a. Municipal Accountant to effect  the  necessary  adjustments  in  the
        books of accounts, to correct  the  noted   erroneous  recording  of
        barangay subprojects, unaccounted PPE item – Swine Multiplier,  non-
        provision and understated depreciation, and to properly classify the
        completed  projects  and  misclassified  items  to  appropriate  PPE
        accounts;



     b. Concerned end-users or recipients of the subject unrecorded  donated
        vehicles to promptly submit to the Municipal Accountant and RSPO the
        IRP or equivalent Property Transfer Report duly supported with  Deed
        of  Donations,  MOA  and  other  applicable  documents,  for  proper
        recording in the property cards and books of accounts;

     c. Municipal Inventory Team timely conduct the  physical  inventory  of
        all its property,  plant  and  equipment,  and  prepare  and  submit
        the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the ensuing  year.   Thereafter,
        the Municipal  Accountant  and  the  Municipal  Inventory  Committee
        should (i) reconcile the balances of the  PPE  per  books  with  the
        inventory reports, (ii) verify and analyze  the discrepancies,  and
        (iii) prepare adjustments to the  affected  records,  in  compliance
        with the procedures prescribed in  Manual  on  the  NGAS  for  LGUs,
        Volume I; and


     d. On the supported accounting records of PPE, the Municipal Accountant
        continue to maintain an updated PPE ledger cards and exert effort to
        determine the details and status of the previous years’  balance  of
        the  Motor  Vehicle,  Construction  and  Heavy  Equipment  and   CIP
        accounts,  such  that  any  necessary  adjustments  to  reflect  the
        accurate balances thereof may be identified and effected.

We  recommended  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  effect  the  proper
adjusting entries to correct the noted erroneous recording  of  transactions
and non-reclassification of completed projects to the appropriate account  –
Market.   Henceforth, ensure a thorough review of recorded transactions  and
analysis  of  details  of  account  balances,  to  prevent   recurrence   of
accounting errors and to reflect accurate account balances in the  financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  effect  the  appropriate
adjustment to correct the remaining unadjusted error in recording the  Loans
Payable account. Henceforth,  observe  the  proper  recording  of  liability
based on loan releases,  recognition  of  service  charges  and  documentary
stamp taxes as deducted from the loan proceeds, and periodic  reconciliation
of loan balances per accounting and bank records, to ensure the  correctness
of Loans Payable amount reflected in the financial statements.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman submit explanation on  the  noted
inconsistencies in the Item Descriptions of the procured  rescue  truck  and
mini backhoe, and on the inspection and acceptance of rescue  vehicle  prior
to actual date of delivery.  Henceforth, the BAC should  observe  compliance
with the procedural guidelines on the preparation of  Bidding  Documents  as
prescribed under Rule VI, Section 17 of the RIRR of RA 9184.

We recommended the following courses of action:


     a. On work items of the  three  projects  not  considered  in  the  COA
        verified  accomplishments  costing  P2,034,242.21,   the   Municipal
        Engineer provide  explanation  on  why  the  necessary  details  and
        technical specifications of such work items discussed above were not
        reflected in the submitted approved plans and specifications;

     b. On defects noted during inspection  amounting  to  P153,718.45,  the
        Municipal Engineer monitor and institute corrective measures on the
        findings of the  COA  RO  IV-B  RTS,  or  in  the  alternative,  the
        Municipal Accountant enforce refund of the cost of deficiencies thru
        deduction from the amount due to respective  contractors  or  demand
        for payment, as the case may be;

     c. On the inclusion of Item 104-Embankment in the detailed estimates of
        project Local Access  Road  Upgrading,  the  Municipal  Engineer  submit
        the  approved Quality Control Program (QCP) including all the
        Material Test Reports particularly with regards to soil, for further
        review and evaluation by the COA RO IV-B RTS; and

     d.  On  variance  between  contract  cost  and  COA  cost  estimate  of
        P1,123,474.62, the Municipal Accountant, in  coordination  with  the
        Municipal Engineer require the concerned contractors to  refund  the
        excess amount of contract cost over COA Cost Estimates, as  may  be
        deemed warranted.

We commended the efforts of the Municipality in  implementing  various
development PPAs.  However, to  fully  attain  the  objective  of  providing
timely socio-economic benefits to its constituents, we reiterated our  prior
year’s  recommendation  that  Management  ensure  the  proper  planning  and
prioritization  of   development   projects,   with   complete   documentary
requirements, to avoid delay in the procurement and implementation process.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:


a. MDRRMO cause the timely implementation  of  the  programs,  projects  and
  activities identified under  the  LDRRMF  to  ensure  resilience  of  the
  Municipal Government and capacity to recover  from  and  respond  to  the
  impacts of disaster and emergencies caused by typhoons, flood, and  other
  natural disasters; and


b. Municipal Accountant comply with the requirements  of  COA  Circular  No.
  2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 on full disclosure of pertinent information
  on DRRM fund in the Notes to the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the GFPS:


     a. continue their efforts in promoting and  ensuring  that  gender  and
        development is mainstreamed in the local policy  making,  planning,
        programming  and  budgeting;  and  implement  all   programmed   GAD
        activities, and  whenever  necessary,  adopt  remedial  measures  to
        ensure the execution of entire GAD PPAs so that desired results  are
        fully achieved; and


     b. strictly observe the reporting guidelines  under  COA  Circular  No.
        2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 on submission to the Audit Team of the
        copy of the GPB within 5 days after  the  receipt  of  its  approved
        plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and other
concerned officials enforce the full settlement of the above suspensions
and take appropriate action against the persons deemed responsible and
liable therefor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. BAC Chairman to continue coordinating with the GPPB TSO, in order to comply with the mandatory posting in the GPPB online portal of the required information relevant to procurement projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under Bayanihan; and b. Municipal Accountant to provide justification on processing the pertinent claims despite the noted deficiencies in the supporting documents; and Henceforth, the BAC and the Municipal Accountant should carefully examine the documentary requirements of bidders or contractors to ensure that such documents are (i) in accordance with the prescribed formats, (ii) properly accomplished, and (iii) contains all mandatory and significant provisions.
observations.managementAction: The BAC informed that they still were unable to access the GPPB online. No action taken. Municipal Accountant did not submit comment on the noted deficiency on OSS
observations.reason: Technical problem on access to portal was not resolved with GPPB TSO.  No justification submitted on the noted incomplete information in the OSS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: BAC Chairman provide satisfactory justification (i) on the procurement of relief goods with values which exceeded the allowable threshold through shopping modality, and (ii) on the deficiencies noted in the supporting documents. Henceforth, the BAC should strictly adhere to the standards set forth in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement process; and Municipal Accountant ensure that claims are supported with complete and valid documentary requirements before processing the payment.
observations.managementAction: BAC Chairman provided satisfactory justification (i) on the procurement of relief goods with values which exceeded the allowable threshold through shopping modality, and (ii) on the deficiencies noted in the supporting documents. On various dates, the Municipal Accountant submitted the monthly BRS for CY2020. The Municipal Treasurer regularly monitor their collection performance, to meet the targeted revenue.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare Officer cause the submission of the lacking documents cited above to the Audit Team in compliance with Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445. Henceforth, to avoid audit suspension and disallowance, ensure the completeness, accuracy, and propriety of documents supporting the claims before processing for payment.
observations.managementAction: On June 02, 11 and 21, 2021, DTRs of employees who claimed hazard pay were submitted from the Office of the Municipal Accountant, HRMO, and MDRRMO, respectively.
observations.reason: Other necessary documents are still to be submitted for evaluation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) prepare and submit the BRS for all of its bank accounts within the timeframe set forth in COA Circular No. 96-011; (ii) effect the appropriate adjustments for all book reconciling items; and (iii) take-up the necessary adjusting entries to revert the total stale checks amounting to P34,680.87. Municipal Treasurer regularly coordinate with and/or request from the AGDB the monthly bank statements or copy of snapshots of cash transactions to facilitate timely reconciliation of CIB-LCCA, and observe the procedural guidelines outlined in Section 59 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I, regarding the cancellation and replacement of stale checks.
observations.managementAction: On various dates, the Municipal Accountant submitted the monthly BRS for CY2020. Bank Statements for CY2020 were made available for preparation of the monthly BRS. The Municipal Treasurer informed the concerned payees regarding the stale checks, although copy of some stale checks were still unfound and could not be presented for cancellation and replacement.
observations.reason: Failure of the Municipal Accountant to take-up adjustments on book reconciling items and stale checks. Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the journal entries for recording receipt of inventories held for distribution and record issuance thereon based on the RIS in order to present an accurate balance of Inventory, Expense or Equity account; and Municipal Inventory Team to conduct a physical count of Inventories every semester and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs. The entries in the RPCI should be reconciled with the accountability recorded in the stock cards of the designated Supply/Property Officer as well as in the Supply Ledger cards of Accounting Office, and necessary adjustments resulting therefrom should be effected in the records, where appropriate. A copy of the RPCI should be submitted to COA on or before July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester respectively.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the issuances based on received RIS, though no adjustment was made on account of recognizing outright expense upon receipt of inventories. Municipal Inventory Team to conduct a physical count of Inventories every semester and prepare the RPCI pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: Absence of RSMI and RPCI, as reference in determining the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the: Municipal Inventory Team conduct complete inventory of all its property, plant and equipment at least annually and prepare and accomplish the RPCPPE. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Inventory Committee should reconcile the balances of the PPE per books with the inventory reports, verify and analyze the discrepancies, and prepare adjustments to the affected records; Municipal Treasurer, through the designated Property Officer record all deliveries of properties in the Property Cards based on the IAR and supporting documents pursuant to Section 119 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and Municipal Accountant update the PPE ledger cards based on the IAR, and take up in the books of accounts the corresponding depreciation of identified PPE accounts to properly reflect the accurate net carrying value of its PPE.
observations.managementAction: The RSPO initiated the preparation and keeping of Property Cards for deliveries during the year 2021. On July 05, 2021, the Municipal Accountant submitted various PPE Ledger cards on Public Infrastructure. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Inventory Team initiated further actions.
observations.reason: The Municipal Inventory Committee was not able to conduct the physical count of all PPE allegedly because the responsible personnel suffered from COVID 19 infection.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant (i) adjust the erroneous recording of loans payable based on appropriation by setting-up unrecorded loans amounting to ₱970,044.41 and ₱6,271,021.04, for SRP and SWMF respectively; (ii) recognize the liability based on actual releases of loan proceeds, and refrain from deducting interest payments in the principal balance of loan, rather record the payment under Interest Expense account; (iii) take-up in the books of accounts the proper adjustment for interest expense erroneously debited in Loans Payable-Domestic account totaling ₱11,418,056.18; and (iv) reverse the entries made for the identified erroneous adjustments totaling ₱18,992,642.25.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recorded the JEV No. 100-2121-12 dated December 29, 2021 to adjust outstanding balance of DFO-MDFO loans. On July 05, 2021, the Municipal Accountant submitted various PPE Ledger cards on Public Infrastructure.
observations.reason: Loans Payable account is still understated by ₱3,005,638.07 which needs further analysis and adjustments. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor undertake appropriate action to comply with the contractual obligation under Sub-Project Loan Agreement with DOF-MDFO particularly on incurred penalty charges amounting to ₱102,225.42; and require the Municipal Accountant to incorporate in the Notes to Financial Statements the proper disclosure on unrecognized contractual commitment arising from the subject loan penalty charges, in conformity with Section 80.3 of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The Management is still reconciling with the MDFO regarding the validity of the imposed penalties on late payment of loan. The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that they are still reconciling with the MDFO.
observations.reason: No payment of penalties or any appropriate action on unsettled penalties for MDFO loan was made. The Municipal Accountant failed to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the pertinent information on this unsettled penalties and unrecognized contractual commitment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the proper planning and prioritization of development project, with proper and complete documentary requirements, to avoid delay in the procurement and implementation process. DILG should pursue the processing of transfer of land title in the name of the Municipality and resolve issues on right of way and other projects to enable completion of the identified development projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government has implemented some of developmental projects under the 20% DF Continuing Appropriation. The Municipal Accountant submitted various PPE Ledger cards on Public Infrastructure and initiated actions to address the items.
observations.reason: Some projects remained unimplemented, while some project are for reversion or realignment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to ensure that the Municipality’s GPB for the ensuing year had a Certificate of Endorsement from the DILG pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-1.
observations.managementAction: The Management provided the Audit Team with a copy of EO No. 16, series of 2020, relevant to the updating of the GAD Focal Point System. Moreover, they asserted that they will course for the timely submission and proper GAD planning and budgeting based on GAD policies.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant (i) require various school beneficiaries with unsubmitted liquidation reports to submit the same to validate how the financial assistance granted to them was spent; and (ii) enforce strict monitoring of submission of liquidation reports to ensure that all financial assistance are fully liquidated at year-end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted liquidation reports for the donations granted during CY 2019 totaling ₱241,040.00. Further, the Management averred that they will withhold the processing of disbursement vouchers pertaining to approved requests of the school-beneficiaries that still have unliquidated financial grants.
observations.reason: Non-liquidation of cash advances granted during CY2019 totaling ₱13,500.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Municipal Assessor (i) conduct continuous ocular inspections at the barangay level to validate the identity and exact addresses of declared owners and update the Assessment Rolls, if necessary; (ii) communicate with the Registrar of Deeds to facilitate the submission of the required abstract of registry for updating of Rolls; and (iii) coordinate with the DENR and DAR to seek information as to the identity of the beneficiaries and new owners of the transferred properties, have them declared for assessment of the local assessor or updating in the Rolls, and Municipal Engineer for the regular transmittal of the copy of the building permits within 30 days from issuance as basis for the assessment of tax on the real properties with the new improvements; and Municipal Treasurer sustain efforts to continuously improve the collection efficiency both of current taxes due and delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor informed the Audit Team that they have already made the necessary representations with the DAR to secure the list of beneficiaries of the CARP. The Municipal Treasurer regularly monitor their collection performance, to meet the targeted revenue.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor’s Office is still waiting for the distribution of titles to CARP beneficiaries. They further asserted that their Office is planning to coordinate with the Registry of Deeds in order to secure a copy of the Abstract of Registry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. BAC Secretariat provide proof of compliance of the posting of the ITB at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality pursuant to Section 21.2.1(a) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; b. Municipal Engineer ensure that the Certificate of Acceptance is issued after the lapse of the defects liability period or one year from project completion up to final acceptance pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184; c. Municipal Accountant (i) submit the CAF for the projects, and (ii) ensure that all progress billings and final payments are recognized as Construction in Progress pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Municipal School Board (i) strictly comply with Item 5 particularly Section 5.5 of DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1 2017 dated January 19, 2017 which provides the guidelines and schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF budget, (ii) require different schools for the earlier submission of their requests for consolidation and consideration in the preparation of SEF budget, and (iii) prepare and submit the PPMP for supplies and materials and prepare a consolidated APP, copy furnish the Audit Team; and b. Municipal Accountant (i) require various school beneficiaries with unsubmitted liquidation reports to submit the same to validate how the financial assistance granted to them was spent and thereafter, enforce strict monitoring of submission of liquidation reports to ensure that all financial assistance are fully liquidated at year-end; and (ii) record the acquisition of the construction materials and other inventory items first to its proper inventory account and to proper expense account upon its issuance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that all cash advances granted to an elective official are for travel purposes only as prescribed under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997; (ii) validate the propriety of the purpose of cash advance of the Municipal Mayor; (iii) ensure the complete documentation in support of its liquidation; and (iv) submit the liquidation reports with supporting documents for the cash advances granted to the Municipal Mayor; b. concerned officials and employees who attended the abovementioned trainings to refund the excess travel claims to avoid disallowance in audit; and c. Municipal Mayor require the officials and employees that all travel claims are reviewed by the immediate supervisors to ensure that travel is necessary and reasonable and consistent with the existing rules and regulations.

The Municipal Accountant assured the Team to comply with the COA Circular and gather the necessary documents to support the claims.
observations.reason: To date, documents relative to the cash advances made for the Intelligence Expenses of the Municipality were not yet submitted to the Audit Team.

Management establish in the books of accounts, if applicable, procured agricultural goods and tools which are awaiting distribution to the beneficiaries of the ICFP Project, ensure that all payments for procured items for distribution are entered in the books of accounts as inventory/supply for distribution, and ensure that Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders are dated and numbered before entering into contracts and processing payments respectively; b. Management provide a satisfactory explanation on the selection of an ICFP Coordinator as and ICFP beneficiary; and c. The ICFP Team members and BAC members submit legal bases on the claim of honoraria.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

Municipal Engineer (i) monitor and institute corrective measures on the findings of the COA TAS, (ii) submit the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit of the project, (iii) strictly comply with the provisions of the General Conditions of the Contract (GCC) particularly Section 40.1 (Progress Payments) thereof, and DPWH Department Order No. 103 dated November 8, 1988 on Calibrated Actions on Contracts in terms of resumption and completion; b. Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to Karangyan Builders and Traders the cost deficiency and institute appropriate action to compel the contractor to pay the amount; and c. Management strictly observe the procurement and contracting procedures to avoid errors and irregularities.

Municipal Engineer submit the certification/documentation on the general obsolescence of the transport and heavy equipment, the availability of spare parts, and repair and maintenance requirements as required by Item 4.5 of DBM NBC No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 in subsequent purchases of heavy equipment; b. BAC Secretariat ensure compliance with Section 20.2 of the Revised IRR of the RA No. 9184 in the holding or conduct of a pre-procurement conference and Section 21.2.1(a) of the law in the posting of the ITB at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality; and c. Municipal Accountant (a) submit the CAF for the purchase of backhoe and garbage truck, and (b) reclassify the purchase of backhoe and garbage truck to Construction and Heavy Equipment account pursuant to Item 5.5.3 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

Municipal Engineer monitor and institute corrective measures on the findings of the COA TAS, (ii) submit the lacking documents to avoid suspension in audit of the project, (iii) strictly comply with the provisions of the General Conditions of the Contract (GCC) particularly Section 40.1 (Progress Payments) thereof, and DPWH Department Order No. 103 dated November 8, 1988 on Calibrated Actions on Contracts; and b. Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to contractors the cost deficiency and institute appropriate action to compel the contractor to pay the amount, as the case maybe.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. BAC Secretariat and Municipal Engineer submit the lacking documents to support the procurement activities of the Municipality; b. Municipal Accountant (i) submit the CAF for the projects, and (ii) ensure that all progress billings and final payments are recognized as Construction in Progress pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009; c. Municipal Engineer provide report on the cost and status of on-going projects and submit detailed cost of components of infrastructure projects; and d. Municipal Accountant reclassify the cost of the materials and labor for the installation of solar streetlights under Power Supply System account to Repair and Maintenance – Infrastructure Asset (Power System) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and analyze the Registry of Public Infrastructure to comply with Item 3.7 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016 on the recognition of the 25% target implementation of the Public Infrastructures in the books of accounts.

The Municipal Accountant informed the Audit team that various JEVs were prepared to reverse the derecognition of Public Infrastructure Assets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: a. Municipal Inventory Team in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer (i) conduct an inventory of the local roads under the Municipality’s jurisdiction and reconcile the list of inventory per local road inventory report and road map with those recorded in the Registry of Public Infrastructure Roads, (ii) maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and (iii) keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; b. Municipal Accountant (i) prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card respectively, (ii) support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructure Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for the road components with no available cost per registry; and (iii) keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and c. Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Team with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects identified, and prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. BAC Chairman to continue coordinating with the GPPB TSO, in order to comply with the mandatory posting in the GPPB online portal of the required information relevant to procurement projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act, if warranted, and submit satisfactory explanation on the noted incomplete stipulations in the supplier’s OSS and incomplete information in procurement documents; and
b. Municipal Accountant to provide justification on processing the pertinent claims despite the noted deficiencies in the supporting documents.
Henceforth, the BAC and the Municipal Accountant should carefully examine the documentary requirements of bidders or contractors to ensure that such documents are (i) in accordance with the prescribed formats, (ii) properly accomplished, and (iii) contains all mandatory and significant provisions.

We recommended that the:
a. MSWD Officer submit to COA the distribution list of relief goods during the community quarantine, supported with copy of requisition and Issue Slips;
b. BAC Chairman provide satisfactory justificationon the procurement of relief goods with values which exceeded the allowable threshold through shopping modality, and on the deficiencies noted in the supporting documents. Henceforth, the BAC should strictly adhere to the standards set forth in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure transparency and accountability in the procurement process; and
c. Municipal Accountant ensure that claims are supported with complete and valid documentary requirements before processing the payment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
a. The Municipal Accountant (i) prepare and submit the BRS for all of its bank accounts within the timeframe set forth in COA Circular No. 96-011,(ii) effect the appropriate adjustments for all book reconciling items, and (iii) take-up the necessary adjusting entries to revert the total stale checks amounting to ₱34,680.87; and
b. Municipal Treasurer regularly coordinate with and/or request from the AGDB the monthly bank statements or copy of snapshots of cash transactions to facilitate timely reconciliation of CIB-LCCA, and observe the procedural guidelines outlined in Section 59 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume Iregarding the cancellation and replacement of stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments to correct the journal entries for recording receipt of inventories held for distribution and record issuance thereon based on the RIS in order to present an accurate balance of Inventory, Expense or Equity account; and
b. Municipal Inventory Team to conduct a physical count of Inventories every semester and prepare the RPCI pursuant to the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs. The entries in the RPCI should be reconciled with the accountability recorded in the stock cards of the designated Supply/Property Officer as well as in the Supply Ledger cards of Accounting Office, and necessary adjustments resulting therefrom should be effected in the records, where appropriate. A copy of the RPCI should be submitted to COA on or before July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.

We reiterated our prior years recommendation that the:
a. Municipal Inventory Team conduct complete inventory of all its PPE at least annually and prepare and accomplish the RPCPPE. The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Inventory Committee should reconcile the balances of the PPE per books with the inventory reports, verify and analyze the discrepancies, and prepare adjustments to the affected records;
b. Municipal Treasurer, through the designated Property Officer, record all deliveries of properties in the Property Cards based on the IAR and supporting documents pursuant to Section 119 of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Municipal Accountant update the PPE ledger cards based on the IAR, and take up in the books of accounts the corresponding depreciation of identified PPE accounts to properly reflect the accurate net carrying value of its PPE.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant:
a. adjust the erroneous recording of loans payable based on appropriation by setting-up unrecorded loans amounting to ₱970,044.41 and ₱6,271,021.04, for SRP and SWMF, respectively; and reversing the set-up of MPH-CPM loans payable amounting to ₱2,200,000.00;
b. take-up in the books of accounts the proper adjustment for interest expense erroneously debited in Loan Payable-Domestic account totaling ₱11,418,056.18; and
c. reverse the entries made for the identified erroneous adjustments totalingP18,992,642.25.
Henceforth, recognize the liability based on actual releases of loan proceeds, and refrain from deducting interest payments in the principal balance of loan, rather record the payment under Interest Expense account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. undertake appropriate action to comply with the contractual obligation under Sub-Project Loan Agreement with DOF-MDFO particularly on incurred penalty charges amounting to ₱102,225.42; and
b. require the Municipal Accountant to incorporate in the Notes to Financial Statements the proper disclosure on unrecognized contractual commitment arising from the subject loan penalty charges, in conformity with Section 80.3 of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs.

We commended the effort of the Municipality for implementing various development projects beneficial to its constituents. However, to fully attain its objective in providing timely socio-economic benefits to its constituents, we recommended that Management ensure the proper planning and prioritization of development project, with proper and complete documentary requirements, to avoid delay in the procurement and implementation process. Management should pursue the processing of transfer of land title in the name of the Municipality and resolve the issues on right of way and other projects to enable completion of the 12 identified development projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point System Executive Committee and Technical Working Group to ensure that the Municipality’s GPB for the ensuing year had a Certificate of Endorsement from the DILG pursuant to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and other concerned officials enforce the settlement of the above suspensions and take appropriate action against the persons deemed responsible and liable therefor.

We recommended that the:

a. Municipal Accountant establish in the books of accounts, if applicable, procured agricultural goods and tools which are awaiting distribution to the beneficiaries of the ICFP Project, ensure that all payments for procured items for distribution are entered in the books of accounts as inventory/supply for distribution, and see to it that all Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders are dated and numbered before entering into contracts amd processing payments respectively;

b. Management provide a satisfactory explanation on the selection of an ICFP Coordinator as and ICFP beneficiary; and

c. The ICFP Team members and BAC members submit legal bases on the claim of honoraria.

d. The ICFP Chairman explain the delay in the implementation of project and utilization of fund; and ensure that the ICFP Project serves its true purpose which is to mitigate hunger of the participating poor families.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

a. through the Liga ng mga Barangay encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the Municipality by educating their respective constituents through Information Education Campaign (IEC) on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the importance of their participation in the implementation various SWM programs;





b. through the MENRO, comply with the requirements of RA No. 9003 on waste segregation by (i) placing trash bins with proper labels on the designated garbage collection area and other public places and collect only those wastes which are properly segregated, (ii) ensure that the existing MRF in Barangay and the LGU’s MRF are functional and the purpose of establishment of MRF is being served, and (iii)  provide the necessary support in enjoining the remaining seven barangays in establishing their own MRF; and




c. see to it the operation of the present dumping site complied with Section 2 of Rule XIII of the IRR of RA No. 9003 until its closure.
observations.managementAction:
2018
AAR Finding
No. 17 | a. In a letter dated February 26, 2019, the designated MENRO informed that (a) they are doing necessary actions to comply with the requirements of RA No. 9003 on waste segregation and its proper implementation; (b) they held meeting on: (i) February 8, 2019 regarding the Solid Waste Management Programs: Proper Segregation of Waste, Construction of MRF both LGU and within Barangays and placing trash bins in designated public places; (ii) February 21, 2019 regarding on the different classification/category of waste and planned the schedule of waste collection according to classification;  appointment of marshal/committee responsible for the monitoring of waste segregation and collection; IEC like pamphlet was also conceptualized to be distributed in every households, institutions and establishments especially in the pilot areas of Poblacion, Ilag, Calsapa, Lumangbayan and Tacligan; (c) Tentative schedule of meeting with barangays of Bigaan, Caagutayan and Calangatan will be held on March 5-7, 2019; (d) requested trash bins will be placed in public area; (e) Presentation of SWM Programs and Municipal Ordinance enacting the Comprehensive Solid Waste Management will be presented in Public hearing/consultation on March 4, 2019; 

In a Letter dated February 26, 2020, the designated MENRO informed that in the later part of 2019, the Municipality distributed IEC materials in households, establishments, public markets, schools and private institutions. Schedule of collections, kinds of waste to be collected and respective fines were posted in the publis places. The waste segregation program also encourages the use of ecobags instead of plastic bags, hence the simultaneous implementation of No Plastic Policy. He further informed that as of February 2020, the implementation of the program was said to be successful since people’s cooperation has been observed.

In a letter dated February 26, 2020, the designated MENRO informed that the Office of the Mayor in coordination with the team of Municipal Environment and Natural Resources conceptualized the production of organic fertilizer from the collected biodegradable materials as an alternative plan to achieve zero waste. Aside from this, Barangay Captains were also encouraged to construct MRF in their respective barangays. This aims to further spread the people’s awareness regarding proper segregation of solid wastes and to help minimize the volume of waste to be collected in every barangay. 

The designated MENRO submitted picture of the              on-going constructions of MRF in Barangay Ilag, Poblacion, Calsapa, Tacligan, Calangatan, Caagutayan, Bigaan and Lumangbayan.

Implemented the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan for the disposal facility.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
a. Management institute measures to facilitate the preparation of the
Bank Reconciliation Statements and other accounting functions; and
b. Municipal Accountant strictly comply with COA Circular No. 96-011
on the submission of the BRS for all the accounts within the prescribed
period and coordinate with the bank for the prompt release of bank
statements to ensure the timely preparation and submission of the BRS
to the Office of the Auditor.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal
Inventory Team conduct complete inventory of all its property, plant and
equipment. In the conduct thereof, the Inventory Team consider (i) proper
planning such as preparation of a plan specifying the date and timeframe of
physical inventory; and (ii) previous inventory reports and the list of PPEs per
type/category from the accounting office be made available for ready reference.
Furthermore, we recommended that the:
a. Municipal Inventory Team (i) prepare and accomplish the RPCPPE in
accordance with Section 57 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs,
Volume II; and (ii) reconcile the results of the count with property and
accounting records;
b. Municipal Treasurer continue the preparation and maintenance of
property cards for PPE acquired from CY 2012 and earlier pursuant to
Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) maintain PPE Ledger Cards for the properties
acquired and recorded in the books from CY 2017 and earlier; (ii)
reverse the JEV made to derecognize Public Infrastructure accounts
completed in CY 2018, (iii) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in
the recognition of the 75% target implementation of Public
Infrastructure accounts in the books of accounts; and (iv) review the
Communication Equipment account and the corresponding
Accumulated Depreciation account and make necessary adjustment to
reflect the correct carrying value of the said PPE account.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Accountant:
a. draw a Journal Entry Voucher to reflect the unrecorded loan releases of
P2,852,351.33 for the Subaan River Protection Dike and P8,231,188.41 for
the Solid Waste Management Facility with Equipment;
b. reverse the entries made for the adjustment of P2,408.15 and P337,977.86
to cover the negative balance of the account, set up of loan releases totaling
P3,842,475.29, adjustment of P3,374,376.95 for the understatement of loan
in 2011, and set-up of loans payable amounting to P2,200,000.00;
c. effect the adjustment of interest expense erroneously debited in Loan
Payable-Domestic account totaling P9,809,916.72;
d. obtain with the MDFO the loan balances as of year-end for the projects
Subaan River Protection Dike and Solid Waste Management Facility as
guide to establish the correct balance of unpaid loans; and
e. maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for loans payable and interest
payments for every loan obtained to facilitate monitoring and detection of
unrecorded loan releases, principal and interest payments.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. analyse and make appropriate adjustments on the noted erroneous
entries of the loans set-up, BEMONC collections, and health employees
payroll;
b. present to the Audit Team the ledger of the Trust Fund – Regular books
and submit the vouchers for October to December 2018 to validate the
correctness and propriety of entries relative to the Due to Other Funds
account, and make necessary adjustments to reflect its correct
reciprocal entry;
c. prepare a reconciliation report of the reciprocal accounts and take up
the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all
reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
d. perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to prevent error
and omissions.

We recommended that the (a) Municipal Government officials and members
of the Local Development Council evaluates the present social, economic and
environmental situations in the locality and appraise the socio-economic and other
development programs and projects to be prioritized and included in the
Development Plan and those to be implemented during the year, and evaluates the
viability of the projects to be implemented to immediately address the needs of the
constituents; and (b) Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the
implementation of those projects to maximize the utilization of the 20% Local
Development Fund in accordance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the:
a. MDRRMO and Municipal Engineer prioritize, monitor, and implement
the programs, projects and activities identified to ensure resilience of
the Municipal Government and capacity to recover from and respond to
the impacts of disaster and emergencies caused by typhoons, flood,
and other natural disasters;
b. Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and
analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed
projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus; and
c. Municipal Accountant transfer and record the unexpended/unobligated
balance of LDRRMF of P2,432,693.41 in the current year to Trust
Liability-LDRRM account in the Trust Fund.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Secretariat provide proof of compliance of the posting of the ITB at
any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality pursuant to
Section 21.2.1(a) of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
b. Municipal Engineer ensure that the Certificate of Acceptance is issued
after the lapse of the defects liability period or one year from project
completion up to final acceptance of the project pursuant to Section
62.2.2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
c. Municipal Engineer, BAC Chairman, and/or Municipal Accountant
submit all the lacking documentary requirements to support the
procurement activities for the above projects to avoid suspension in
audit; and
d. Municipal Accountant (i) submit the CAF for the projects, and (ii)
ensure that all progress billings and final payments are recognized as
Construction in Progress pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer submit the certification/documentations on the
general obsolescence of the transport and heavy equipment, the
availability of spare parts, and the quality, degree and extent of repair
and maintenance requirements and ensure to comply with Item 4.5 of
DBM NBC No. 446-A dated January 30, 1998 in subsequent purchases
of heavy equipment;
b. BAC Secretariat ensure compliance with Section 20.2 of the Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184 in the holding or conduct of a pre-procurement
conference and Section 21.2.1(a) of the law in the posting requirement of
the ITB at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality;
and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) submit the CAF for the purchase of bakhoe
and garbage truck, and (ii) reclassify the purchase of backhoe and
garbage truck to Construction and Heavy Equipment account pursuant
to Item 5.5.3 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March
25, 2013 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the:
a. Management provide satisfactory explanation on the unusual work
suspension of 123 calendar days and on the issuance of suspension order
to give time to an activity which is already considered in the
determination of the original contract time;
b. Municipal Engineer submit a copy of the revised PERT/CPM/Network
Diagram and copy of the approved original plans indicating the affected
portion(s) of the project and duly revised plans and specifications, if
applicable, indicating the changes made which shall be color coded; and
c. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit all the lacking
documentary requirements to support the procurement activities of the
Municipality and avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the:
a. Management render satisfactory explanation on the (i) failure to allocate
LGU counterpart for the procurement of the Flatbed Dryer and Rice
Transplanter as provided in Section 2 (c) of the MOA; and(ii)
inconsistencies and other observations in various supporting documents;
b. Municipal Accountant (i) submit a copy of the signed MOA with the DA
for the implementation of Native Chicken Production and Construction of
Bagsakan Center; and (ii) return the unused funds to the DA as required
in Section 2 (g) of the MOA; and
c. Municipal Engineer, BAC Chairman, and Municipal Accountant submit
all the lacking documentary requirements to support the procurement
activities in the contract for the Construction of Flatbed Dryer and
Construction of Bagsakan Center to avoid suspension in audit.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) submit to the Office of the Auditor for post-
audit the FURs of the amount transferred by DILG for the two Water
System projects, with all supporting vouchers/payrolls and documents
for the 1st and 2nd partial payment for Phase 8 and 2nd and final payment
for Phase 9 and (ii) ensure timely preparation and submission of the
FURs as prescribed in Sections 6.4 and 6.5 of COA Circular No. 94-013;
and
b. Municipal Engineer and BAC Chairman submit a copy of the Duly
Approved Drawings/Plans and Technical Specifications.

We reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the:
a. Municipal School Board (i) strictly comply with Item 5 particularly
Section 5.5 of DepEd, DBM and DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 dated January
19, 2017, which provides the guidelines and schedule of activities in the
preparation and approval of the SEF budget, (ii) require different
schools for the earlier submission of their requests for consolidation and
consideration in the preparation of SEF budget, and (iii) prepare and
submit the PPMP for supplies and materials and prepare a consolidated
APP, copy furnished the Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) require various school beneficiaries with
unsubmitted liquidation report to submit the same to validate how the
financial assistance granted to them was spent and thereafter, enforce
strict monitoring of submission of liquidation reports to ensure that all
financial assistance are fully liquidated at year-end, and (ii) record
the acquisition of the construction materials and other inventory items
first to its proper inventory account and to proper expense account
upon its issuance.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation and the Municipal
Accountant agreed to prepare and submit the Statement of Changes in Net
Assets/Equity and the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer revert
the unexpended balances of appropriations for Maintenance and Other Operating
Expenses for CY 2018 and those remaining CY 2017 unexpended balance in
compliance with Articles 418 and 422 Section (c) of the IRR of the Local
Government Code of 1991 and those unexpended balance of appropriation for
MOOE can be considered savings and part of the unappropriated surplus
available for the next budget year .

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure compliance and
monitor the implementation of the newly approved guidelines in granting of
medical, burial, educational, transportation and other assistance for uniformity
and fairness to avoid possible misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the:
a. Honorable Mayor ensure that the members of the GAD Focal Point
System including elected local officials and department heads undergo
capacity building programs on GAD as enumerated in Section 4.1.A.3 of
PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016; and
b. GAD Focal Point Officer (i) prepare and submit the GPB and GAD AR
by observing the scheduled GAD Planning and Budgeting as provided in
Section 4.1.C.5 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as
amended, to facilitate timely submission to the PPDO and the
subsequent review and endorsement by the DILG Provincial Office and
(ii) attach the results of the HGDG assessment together with the
supporting documents to the GPB and GAD AR for submission.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
c. through the Liga ng mga Barangay encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of
the Municipality by educating their respective constituents through
Information Education Campaign (IEC) on the proper segregation of
reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes and the
importance of their participation in the implementation various SWM
programs;
d. through the MENRO, comply with the requirements of RA No. 9003 on
waste segregation by (i) placing trash bins with proper labels on the
designated garbage collection area and other public places and collect
only those wastes which are properly segregated, (ii) ensure that the
existing MRF in Barangay and the LGU’s MRF are functional and the
purpose of establishment of MRF is being served, and (iii) provide the
necessary support in enjoining the remaining seven barangays in
establishing their own MRF; and
e. see to it that the operation of the present dumping site complied with
Section 2 of Rule XIII of the IRR of RA No. 9003 until its closure.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (i) analyze the unremitted
balance of the Due to GSIS account; (ii) exert time and effort to reconcile the
billings and remittances, and (iii) effect the necessary adjustments and
corrections, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and other
concerned officials enforce the settlement of the above suspensions and
disallowances and take appropriate action against the persons deemed responsible
and liable thereof pursuant to the provisions of the Rules and Regulations on the
Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).

We commended the Offices of the Municipal Accounting and Treasury for
their continuous adherence to revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the
taxes due to the BIR. Further, we however, recommended that the Municipal
Accountant perform analysis of the Due to BIR account so that any discrepancy
may be resolved at earliest opportunity.

We recommended that the Honorable Mayor enjoin the department heads to assist the Municipal Accountant in the implementation of the accounting system and the new chart of accounts in compliance with the requirement of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016, as follows: The Municipal Treasurer provide copies of memorandum of agreements, service contracts and other agreements with respect to deposits of funds and other contracts which affect the accounting of financial assets and liabilities and the management of physical assets of the local government unit; Municipal Engineer provide report on the cost and status of on-going projects and submit detailed cost of components of infrastructure projects. Further, the Municipal Accountant: (i) reclassify the cost of the materials and labor for the installation of solar street lights under Power Supply System to Repair and Maintenance – Infrastructure Asset (Power System) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and (ii) analyze the Registry of Public Infrastructure to comply with Item 3.7 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016, on the recognition of the 25% target implementation of the Public Infrastructures in the books of accounts.
