observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant strictly comply with
COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 regarding the submission of the
BRS for all the accounts within the prescribed period and coordinate with the
bank for the prompt release of bank statements to ensure the timely preparation
and submission of the BRS to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant (i) ensure that all cash advances granted to an
elective official are for travel purposes only as prescribed under COA
Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997, (ii) validate the propriety
of the purpose of cash advance of the Municipal Mayor, (iii) ensure the
complete documentation in support of its liquidation, and (iv) submit
the liquidation reports with supporting documents for the cash
advances of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Accountant; and
the official receipts of the refund of excess cash advances of the
concerned employees; or effect necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts to present fairly the financial statements;
b. concerned officials and employees who attended the abovementioned
trainings to refund the excess travel claims to avoid disallowance in
audit; and
c. Municipal Mayor require the officials and employees that all travel
claims are reviewed by the immediate supervisors to ensure that travel
is necessary and reasonable and consistent with the existing rules and
regulations.
We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant adopt the imprest system for the petty cash transactions and adhere
to the prescribed procedures for the establishment, liquidation, and replenishment
of the PCF pursuant to Section 48 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I.
Moreover, discontinue the practice of charging financial aid in the PCF and
require the Disbursing Officer to draw another cash advance specifically for
financial assistance/aid to indigent.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal
Inventory Team conduct complete inventory of all its property, plant and
equipment. In the conduct thereof, the Inventory Team consider (i) proper
planning such as preparation of a plan specifying the date and timeframe of
physical inventory; and (ii) previous inventory reports and the list of PPEs per
type/category from the accounting office be made available for ready reference.
Furthermore, we recommended that the:
a. Municipal Inventory Team (i) prepare and accomplish the RPCPPE in
accordance with Section 57 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs,
Volume II; and (ii) reconcile the results of the count with property and
accounting records;
b. Municipal Treasurer continue the preparation and maintenance of
property cards for PPE acquired from CY 2012 and earlier pursuant to
Section 114 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
c. Municipal Accountant (i) reclassify the progress billings to the
appropriate Construction in Progress account and all the completed and
fully paid projects to proper PPE accounts; (ii) reverse the JEV made to
derecognize Public Infrastructure accounts completed in CY 2017,
(iii) coordinate with the Municipal Engineer in the recognition of the
50% target implementation of Public Infrastructure accounts in the
books of accounts; and (iv) review the Furniture and Fixture account
and the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation account and make
necessary adjustment to reflect the correct carrying value of the said
PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Review and analyse the entries made to record all the receipts and
utilization of fund transfers received from various national agencies and
effect the appropriate correcting entries to fairly present the financial
statements; and
b. Ensure preparation of the supporting SLs/schedules for each purpose of
fund transfer for each source agency for proper monitoring and
reconciliation purposes.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Accountant:
a. draw a Journal Entry Voucher to reflect the unrecorded loan releases
of P2,852,351.33 for the Subaan River Protection Dike and
P8,231,188.41 for the Solid Waste Management Facility with
Equipment;
b. reverse the entries made for the adjustment of P2,408.15, set up of loan
releases totaling P1,296,161.29, and adjustment of P3,374,376.95 for
the understatement of loan in 2011;
c. effect the adjustment of interest expense erroneously debited in Loan
Payable-Domestic account totaling P8,824,673.00;
d. obtain with the MDFO the loan balances as of year-end for the projects
Subaan River Protection Dike and Solid Waste Management Facility
as guide to establish the correct balance of unpaid loans;
e. maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for loans payable and interest
payments for every loan obtained to facilitate monitoring and detection
of unrecorded loan releases, principal and interest payments; and
f. provide copy of JEV No. 3949-1 to establish the nature and propriety
of the entries made.
For future related transactions, recognize the liability based on actual
releases, and refrain from deducting interest payments in the principal
balance of loan, rather record the payment under Interest Expense account.

We recommended that the:
a. BAC Secretariat (i) provide proof of compliance of the posting of the
ITB at any conspicuous place in the premises of the Municipality
pursuant to Section 21.2.1 (a) and (b) of the Revised IRR of RA No.
9184; (ii) submit to the Audit Team the lacking documents for audit
purposes; and (iii) ensure that the Municipality abides with the
provisions of RA No. 9184 and its Revised IRR and that all contracts
are duly documented to support the propriety of payments to the
contractor;
b. Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to (1) adhere with
DPWH Department Order No. 101, Series of 2015 dated December 22,
2005, Amendments to Department Order No. 53, Series of 1994; re:
Certificate of Completion and Certificate of Acceptance wherein the
Certificate of Completion is signed by the Municipal Mayor; and (2)
ensure that the Certificate of Acceptance be issued after the lapse of
the defects liability period or one year from project completion up to
final acceptance of the project pursuant to Section 62.2.2 of the Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184; and
c. Municipal Accountant ensure that all progress billings and final
payments are recognized as Construction in Progress pursuant to COA
Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant establish in the books of accounts, if applicable,
procured agricultural goods and tools which are awaiting distribution to the
beneficiaries of the ICFP Project, ensure that all payments for procured
items for distribution are entered in the books of accounts as
inventory/supply for distribution, and see to it that all Purchase
Requests and Purchase Orders are dated and numbered before
entering into contracts and processing payments, respectively;
b. Management provide a satisfactory explanation on the selection of an
ICFP Coordinator as an ICFP beneficiary; and
c. The ICFP Team members and BAC members submit legal bases on the
claim of honoraria.
d. The ICFP Chairman explain the delay in the implementation of project
and utilization of fund; and ensure that the ICFP Project serves its true
purpose which is to mitigate hunger of the participating poor
families.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer submit the approved drawing/plan of the project
and the permit to construct issued by the DOH-Regional Director;
b. Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant submit the lacking
documents to support the procurement activities of the Municipality
and avoid suspension in audit; and
c. BAC Chairman specify the date of issuance of the warranty security.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Accountant and BAC Secretariat submit the disbursement
voucher and other necessary documents relative to the full payment for
the 100% accomplishment and completion of the project for audit
purposes and final inspection and evaluation of the project;
b. BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant abide
by the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, No. 4.7 of GPPB
Circular No. 01-2009 and Item 5.7.2. of the DBM Circular Letter No.
2010-9, and Section 4(6) of Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1445; and
ensure that all contracts are duly documented to support the propriety
of payments to the contractor/s.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal Engineer (i) monitor and institute corrective measures on
the findings of the COA TAS, (ii) submit the lacking documents to
avoid suspension in audit of the project and/or final evaluation of the
project, (iii) strictly comply with the provisions of the General
Conditions of the Contract (GCC) particularly Section 40.1 (Progress
Payments) thereof, and DPWH Department Order No. 102 dated
November 8, 1988 on Calibrated Actions on Contracts with Negative
Slippage, (iv) comment on the technical observations of the COA TAS
in the BUB-NAPC – Concreting of Tanak Road, Lumangbayan,
and/or
b. Municipal Accountant deduct from the amount due to Karangyan
Builders and Traders the cost deficiency of P418,116.66 and A.M.
Tarin Construction and General Merchandise the cost deficiency of
P57,812.48 or institute appropriate action to compel the contractor to
pay the amount, as the case may be.

We recommended that the:
a. Municipal School Board (i) strictly comply with Item 5 of DepEd,
DBM and DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017, inviting
attention to Section 5.5 of the circular which provides the guidelines
and schedule of activities in the preparation and approval of the SEF
budget, and (ii) consolidate all requests for procurement, prepare and
submit the PPMP for supplies and materials, and prepare a
consolidated APP, copy furnished the Audit Team; and
b. Municipal Accountant (i) require the submission of liquidation report
of the Municipality’s support for the 2017 Provincial Meet and the
financial assistance granted to various school beneficiaries to validate
how the assistance was spent, and (ii) record the acquisition of the
construction materials and other inventory items first to its proper
inventory account and to proper expense account upon its issuance.

We recommended that the Municipality’s SWM Board (i) revisit the SWM
Plan; (ii) comply and submit the additional information needed for approval of
the 10-year SWM plan; and (iii) monitor and review the targets and strategies to
ensure efficient management of the Municipality’s SWM program.

We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the SWM Board adopt
measures to mitigate the effects of the current open/controlled dumpsite and to
provide the necessary funds for its proper operation.

We reiterated our previous year recommendation that the Municipal
Accountant prepare the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Accountant and other
concerned officials enforce the settlement of the above suspensions and
disallowances and take appropriate action against the persons deemed responsible
and liable thereof pursuant to the provisions of the Rules and Regulations on the
Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).

We commended the Offices of the Municipal Accounting and Treasury for
their continuous adherence to revenue regulations and prompt remittance of the
taxes due to the BIR. Further, we however, recommended that the Municipal
Accountant perform analysis of the Due to BIR account so that any discrepancy
may be resolved at earliest opportunity.

We recommended and Management agreed that: The Municipal Inventory Team conduct complete inventory of all its property, plant and equipment. Proper planning should be considered such as preparation of a plan specifying the date and timeframe of physical inventory; previous inventory reports and the list of PPEs per type/category from the accounting office must be available for ready reference; Semi-expendable items and unserviceable properties should be identified and segregated from the inventory reports of PPE and must be prepared in a separate report. After the physical inventory taking, the inventory committee shall reconcile the results of the count with property and accounting records. As an initial step, reconciliation between accounting and property records may begin from the current year’s acquisitions/procurement and to select PPE category like motor vehicles, construction and heavy equipment and other categories that are not voluminous; The Municipal Inventory Team in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer compare the property records with the property ledger cards maintained by the Accounting Office; and The Municipal Accountant and the Inventory Team continuously reconcile the balances of the PPE per books with the inventory reports, verify and analyze the discrepancies and prepare adjustments to affected records.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the: The Assistant Municipal Treasurer provide column headings in the record book and additional column to record the date reported the accountable forms was totally used in accordance with Section 95, Book II, Volume I of the GAAM, and prepare the consolidated RAAF to reflect the actual receipts and issuances of accountable forms. The collectors in the market to present or deliver the half torn cash ticket to the Assistant Treasurer for proper monitoring pursuant to Section 74, Book II, Volume I of the GAAM and Section 6A.04, Chapter VI of the 2006 Municipal Revenue Code; All collectors prepare Requisition and Issue Voucher for the requisitions of accountable forms for their use and avoid the transfer of accountable forms between collectors pursuant to Section 25 of COA Circular No. 92-382, and ensure to indicate the correct beginning balances, issuances and ending balance of accountable forms in the Report of Collection and Deposit.

On August 23, 2016, the Municipal Accountant informed COA that the newly created Inventory Team, together with head of offices already convened to formulate plans and actions for the physical count of PPE. The Inventory Team committed to submit partial report to COA. On August 30, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted the partial inventory report for PPE which include the list of motor vehicles and heavy equipment as of December 31, 2015 totaling P5,312,912.00 and Mayor’s Office Inventory of vehicles and office equipment as of August 2016 totaling P4,477,192.00.
observations.reason: Incomplete list and description of property

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer review and verify the reconciling items identified in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank account. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant submit the bank reconciliation statement for Trust Fund-Regular for the period May to December 2015 and ensure that BRS are submitted regularly within 10 days from receipt of the bank statements pursuant to COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: On August 22, 2016, the Municipal Accountant requested the bank to furnish them copy of their bank statements covering the period May 2015 to July 2016. On various dates, the Municipal accountant submitted the May to December 2015 BRS. The interest income earned was already effected in the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the loan proceeds and interest payment to its proper account in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015; and exert efforts to reconcile the reciprocal accounts Due From Other Funds and Due to Other Funds and (b) review and analyze the causes of discrepancies and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: On August 22, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2016-08-2763-1 to take up adjustment for completed Solid Waste Facility debiting Other Infrastructure Assets and crediting Due from NGAs in the amount of P7,946,502.41. On August 23, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2016-08-2763-2 to take up adjustment affecting Receivables account under GF in the amount of P135,062.89.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that: (i) The Municipal Government officials and members of the Local Development Council properly plan, prioritize all activities, and appraise the socio-economic development programs and projects to be implemented during the year, and evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional funds needed to complete a project programmed in the previous years; and (ii) the Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of those projects to avoid delays and maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Local Development Fund in accordance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011. Moreover, the Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160; and the Municipal Engineer periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, and submit a list of all completed projects to support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: On August 30, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted the explanations and justification by the MEO and MPDO on the non-implementation of some projects under the 20% development fund. On August 23, 2016, the Municipal Accountant informed that the unexpended balances totaling P24,692.79 from completed projects were reviewed and proposed to be reverted following the procedures for reversion of the unexpended balance. The Management did not submit any comment on the matter.
observations.reason: Some of the projects were not implemented due to reasons cited in the status of implementation of the 20% development fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: The BAC Chairman explain (i) the issuance of Purchase Order to Solewind Builders beyond the 10 day-period after receipt of Notice of Award; (ii) the need to execute contract agreement and purchase order for the same transaction; (iii) why the Municipality allow Solewind Builders to deliver the 56 units of LED solar street lamp with steel post and solar panel on a staggered basis; and The Municipal Accountant explain (i) why no Certificate of Availability Fund (CAF) was issued; (ii) the acceptance of the Surety Bond in support of the claim for 15% advance payment of Solewind Builders instead of the irrevocable letter of credit or bank guarantee issued by a Universal or Commercial Bank; and (iii) the non-imposition of liquidated damages for the delay incurred by Solewind Builders in delivering the 56 units of LED solar street lamp with steel post and solar panel. Further, the Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman and Municipal Engineer agreed to submit the lacking documents and ensure to indicate the dates of acceptance of the Property Officer and inspection of the Inspection Officers in the AIR and the date of the acknowledgement of receipt by the supplier of the Notice of Award (NOA), Purchase Order (PO) and Notice to Proceed (NTP).
observations.managementAction: In his letter dated August 24, 2016, the BAC Chairman provided explanation on the observations regarding the (i) the issuance of Purchase Order to Solewind Builders beyond the 10 day-period after receipt of Notice of Award; (ii) the need to execute contract agreement and purchase order for the same transaction; (iii) why the Municipality allow Solewind Builders to deliver the 56 units of LED solar street lamp with steel post and solar panel on a staggered basis. The Municipal Accountant submitted the certificate of availability of fund, justification as regards the acceptance of surety bond, and the letter informing Solewind for the forfeiture of retention money to answer for the liquidated damages.
observations.reason: For evaluation of the documents submitted to consider the forfeiture of retention money to cover the liquidated damages.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant explain (a) the recording of streetlights in the power supply system instead to a more appropriate PPE account; (b) the direct debit of the 15% advance payment to the PPE account instead of classifying as prepayment account subject to recoupment; and (c) the non-capitalization of the cost of labor and incidental expenses incurred in the installation of 90 sets of LED solar streetlights.
observations.managementAction: On August 15, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted JEV # 100-2016-08-2740-01 dated August 15, 2016 to take up adjustment for capitalized cost related to LED solar streetlights amounting to P108,525.00 and on August 23, 2016 JEV#100-2016-08-2755 dated August 19, 2016 to reclassify the streetlight from Power Supply System to Land Improvement account amounting to P4,110,227.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) trace and account for the (i) difference of P526,379.46 between unexpended balance of QRF and MOOE and the amount transferred under the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Fund (5-02-15-010), (ii) difference of P1,119,966.74 between the beginning balance of continuing appropriation for capital outlay in the General Fund and the audited balance, and (iii) unrecorded expenditures of P774,933.00 in the Trust Fund books; prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balance of the LDRRMF; present the actual expenditures for the month in the Report of Sources and Utilization of Fund; and reclassify the payment for the partial and final payment for the Construction of Evacuation Center and partial payment for the Rehabilitation of the Slope Protection at Barangay Paspasin to appropriate Construction in Progress account. The Municipal Budget explain the excess charges under Capability Building Program; and the MDRRMO prioritize and implement the programs, projects and activities identified to ensure resilience of the Municipal Government and capacity to recover from and respond to the impacts of disaster and emergencies caused by typhoons, flood, and other natural disasters. Furthermore, ensure that such projects are with adequate funds.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the revised LDRRMFIP to show the list of projects and activities to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years. The revised LDRRMFIP shows Trust Fund - MOOE DRRMSupplies P39,258.48; Assistance to Victims of Disasters P250,000.00; Trust Fund – Capital Outlay Construction of Slope Protection P1,180,000.00; Construction of Evacuation Center P1,400,000.00; TOTAL P2,869,258.48. The Municipal Budget Officer informed that based on their office record, the Charges under the Capability Building Program of LDRRMF for FY 2016 did not exceed its appropriation of P250,000.00. She also stated that the LDRRMF Utilization Report was not prepared by their office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman and Municipal Engineer submit the lacking documents to support the payments made by the Municipality relative the Construction of Evacuation Center Phase I.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted some of the documents enumerated in the AOM on various dates.
observations.reason: The failure of the contractor to submit the Bid Data Sheet and Certification from the PTO on the Sand Gravel Tax paid by the contractors.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the BAC Chairman and Municipal Accountant agreed to submit the documents lacking in support to the claim to avoid suspension in audit.
observations.managementAction: On various dates, the Municipal Accountant submitted copies of PO, NOA, Contract, NTP, Philgeps Posting of NOA & NTP, OR payment of bid documents, Instruction to Bidders, Performance Bond. On January 4, 2017, the Municipal Accountant submitted the copy of LTO Registration of fire truck with Official Receipt No. 1054077415 dated 12.22.2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reclassify the payment of P295,000.00 of fire truck from Other Machinery & Equipment to account Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment, prepare the subsidiary ledger for the Firefighting Equipment and Accessories, and ensure the recording of transactions in the appropriate account pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units under COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: On August 23, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted Journal Entry Voucher #100-16-08-2754 dated August 19, 2016 to record the receipt of the asset account from TF-LDRRMF in the GF books. On August 18, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted JEV #400-16-08-2740 dated August 17, 2016 reclassifying the purchase of second partial payment of fire truck from Other Machinery and Equipment to Disaster & Rescue Equipment for P295,000.00 in the TF books.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, BAC Chairman and Municipal Engineer submit the lacking documents to support the procurement activities of the Municipality relative the Salintubig Project. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant explain the release of retention money upon substitution of surety bond which is not equal to the retention money.
observations.managementAction: On August 15, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted copies of Bid Data Sheet, copy of OR for the purchase of bid documents, legal, technical and financial documents, Philgeps registration and BIR tax clearance of winning bidder, CAF, Inspection report of the Engineer, Statement of Time elapsed, certificate of completion and acceptance and approved as-built plans.
observations.reason: For evaluation of the documents submitted and to consider the explanation on the release of retention money upon substitution of surety bond not equal to the retention money.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MSWDO and Municipal Accountant to submit the Memorandum of Agreement or undertakings or similar documents between the Municipality of San Teodoro and the DSWD relative to the livelihood assistance to 35 residents affected by Typhoon Yolanda to enable the audit team to ascertain the propriety of the disbursement.
observations.managementAction: On August 15, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted copy of MOA between the Municipality and DSWD.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reclassify the shelter assistance to Donations (5-02-99-080) account; honorarium of resource persons and meals and snacks incurred for seminars to Training Expenses (5-02-02-010); cash prizes for contests, competitions, and pageants to account Prizes (5-02-06-020); municipal aid to barangays to Subsidy to Other Local Government Units (5-02-14-030); and cost of printing of newspaper to Printing and Publication Expense (5-02-99-020). Further, the Budget Officer review the Budget for succeeding years covering lump sum appropriations to OMOE, providing details thereon and defining those to be budgeted under specific expenditures in accordance with COA Circular 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
observations.managementAction: On August 23, 2016, the Municipal Accountant submitted various Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) issued in 2016 to show the use of proper accounts in recording various transactions. The Management committed to use proper and specific account for expenses that can be identified and refrain from using repeatedly the OMOOE account.
observations.reason: The non-definition and classification of all the expenses to be charged in the OMOOE in the Annual Budget for the year 2016 .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, as Chairman of the Inventory Team, monitor and supervise the ongoing physical count of the Municipality’s property, and prepare and submit the report upon its completion; and the Municipal Accountant update the list or record of PPE to effect the reconciliation between the property records with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared and submitted the inventory of municipal properties on July 9, 2015. There is an ongoing inventory taking per offices. The Office of the Municipal Accountant is also conducting physical count of properties outside LGU building, simultaneous with the physical count of barangay properties.
observations.reason: The inventory report does not provide relevant information such as acquisition date of properties. The inventory is prepared by matching the properties purchased per voucher as against the submitted list of properties held by different offices and not through physical count. Further, the inventory team did not actively participate in the conduct of such undertaking. The lack of manpower and office workload prohibits completion of inventory.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit an updated, detailed and official copy of the List/Schedule of all its PPE with complete data/information particularly the acquisition date and cost as basis of the computation of depreciation of each PPE item; and support with appropriate schedules the JEV for any adjustments or corrections made.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant started working on the list required. The Municipal Accountant submitted the List of Properties as of December 2014 in the mid of CY 2015. The Municipal Accountant is working on the schedule required.
observations.reason: The tasks were not completed due to lack of manpower. The Accounting Office aside from the head of Office has only 3 permanent staff and 1 job order employee. One staff has optionally retired and the other staff was transferred/promoted to Office of the HRMO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal officials and members of the Local Development Council plan, prioritize all activities, and appraise the socio-economic development programs and projects to be implemented during the year; evaluate viability of the projects as well as the need for any additional funds needed to complete a project programmed in the previous years; and the Municipal Engineer coordinate, monitor and evaluate the implementation of those projects to avoid delays and maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Local Development Fund in accordance with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.
observations.managementAction: The municipality implemented the 5.5M projects from the 20% Development Fund of CY 2014. The other projects were reverted/reprogrammed. On August 23, 2016, the Municipal Accountant informed that the unexpended balances totaling P24,692.79 from completed projects were reviewed and proposed to be reverted following the procedures for reversion of the unexpended balance. The Management did not submit any comment on the matter.
observations.reason: The implementation of projects under the 20% development fund were delayed due to other national funded projects, thus affecting the total completion of projects under the fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant carried the remaining balances to the following year for the charging of expenses; review and analyze the unexpended balances of appropriations of completed projects for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in accordance with Section 322 of RA 7160; and the Municipal Engineer periodically provide the status of projects undertaken by the Municipality as basis of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant in their periodic review and monitoring of balances of continuing appropriations, and submit a list of all completed projects to support the reversion of such unexpended balance to the unappropriated surplus.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant carried the remaining balances to the following year for the charging of expenses.
observations.reason: The remaining balances of completed projects were used to augment labor payroll and materials for Construction of Day Care Center and Improvement of waterworks, respectively.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the designated Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer facilitate the completion of the Municipal SWM plan for submission to and approval by the NSWMC.
observations.managementAction: The municipality submitted the 10-year SWM Plan 2015-2024 to the Environmental Management Bureau, DENR, Region IV-B through the Provincial Environment and Natural Resources Officer on September 1, 2015. On June 28, 2016, the NSWMC issued Resolution No. 726 approving the 10-year SWM plan of the Municipality of San Teodoro subject to the submission of additional requirements and information as specified in the evaluation checklist provided.
observations.reason: The approval of the 10-year SWM Plan is subject to submission of additional requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management effectively implement RA 9003 in the Municipality through developing and adopting policies and ordinances designed to influence the public to participate and promote solid waste management, and find possible use of shredded plastics so as to divert waste into a usable state.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan is pushing for the enactment of Ordinances that would promote solid waste management in the municipality. The Municipality cannot fully implement the policies embedded in the plan since the approval of the 10-year SWM Plan is subject to submission of additional requirement by the NSWMC.
observations.reason: The approval of the 10-year SWM Plan is subject to submission of additional requirement by the NSWMC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MSWDO prepare and submit the list of plantilla positions with the corresponding job description/actual duties that describes the basis for justifying such positions who are exposed to occupational risks and assigned to hazardous places described under Section 16.1.1 of the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9433, and cause the approval of such list by the Honorable Mayor; and attach to the payroll for hazard pay copy of the DTR, and approved leave of absence as certified by the Human Resource Management Officer. In addition, establish procedures in identifying the personnel assigned to hazardous areas and exposed to occupational risk and duration of their exposure and thereafter submit it for approval of the Mayor.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted the list of plantilla personnel with its actual duties and exposure to risk, and the basis or justification entitling them to hazard pay as approved by the Local Chief Executive, along with certification as to risks exposure entitling them to received hazard pay as stipulated under Magna Carta for Social Workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant explain the difference of P22,425.00 as subsistence and laundry allowances between the Status of Appropriation, Allotment, Obligations and Balances (SAAOB) and the Statement of Income and Expenses; The MGADH-I–Personnel Officer direct the staff who prepares the payroll for subsistence and laundry allowances of the employees of the Municipal Health Office to consider No. 8.2 and 10.2 of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No 1, series of 2012 dated November 29, 2012; and The Municipal Accountant submits the documents supporting the paid payrolls for CY 2014 of the Municipal Health Office in order to evaluate the propriety of the full grant of these allowances.
observations.managementAction: Management already considered the actual services on all workdays in a month in the computation of laundry allowances. The Municipal Accountant submits the documents supporting the paid payrolls for CY 2014 of the Municipal Health Office in order to evaluate the propriety of the full grant of these allowances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The BAC Chairman submit the documents supporting the claim of honoraria of the personnel engaged in the procurement activities of the Municipality; and The Municipal Accountant withhold taxes to paid honoraria in compliance with Section 2.78.5 of Revenue Regulation No. 10-2008 dated July 8, 2008.
observations.managementAction: The BAC submitted the required documents. The Municipal Accountant withheld taxes on honoraria for the period January to June 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Budget Officer classify and identify the specific expense accounts to be used under various programs and activities to avoid charging all expenses either to Donations or Other MOOE; and the Municipal Accountant to classify the actual expenses to appropriate expense accounts so as to limit the expenses charged against the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had drawn JEV memo entry to take up reclassification into proper expense accounts of expenses booked as Other MOOE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer instruct the collectors to remit their collections intact and daily before the close of office hours to the Liquidating Officer for turn over to the Municipal Treasurer pursuant to Section 31 of NGAS Manual for LGU Volume I; and deposit intact all collections on the same day or the next banking day to minimize exposure to risk of possible loss pursuant to Section 32 of MNGAS for LGU Volume I.
observations.managementAction: The remittance of collection by collecting officers to Municipal Treasurer is religiously complied daily. The MTO see to it that deposit is made the next day after month end to minimize risk of possible loss. The Municipal Treasurer instructed the collectors to remit their collections intact and daily before the close of office hours.
observations.reason: Deposit of collections is made twice a week due to distance of depository bank.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer review and verify the reconciling items identified in the BRS and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to reflect the correct balances of the Cash in Bank Account and explain the differences noted in the balances of Special Education Fund and PFPR account under the Trust Fund; and The Municipal Accountant submits the original copies of bank statements and paid checks pursuant to COA Circular 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.managementAction: The interest income amounting to P116.89, P121.40 and P4,272.20 has been adjusted as well as disbursement of previously stated check of 1,900.00. The Municipal Accountant submitted JEV memo entry for the transfer of interest amounting to P1,111.60 from TF to GF. Paid checks and bank statements for the year 2014 and 2015 were already submitted to PAO, together with its BRS.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the list of its PPE and draw a Journal Entry Voucher to correct the erroneous classification of the properties in accordance with COA Circular No. 2004-008 dated September 20, 2004.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant corrected the erroneous classification of some PPE items.
observations.reason: Unavailable data on the acquisition dates of property.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipal management and the Municipal Accountant make the necessary revision of the useful life of PPE and prepare an adjusting entry to reflect the correct balances of the PPE account in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The municipality used the Revised Useful Life per COA Circular No. 2003-007 and 2004-005 on some PPE.
observations.reason: Incomplete record of taxpayers for the preparation of list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: (a) The Municipal Treasurer update the Real Property Tax Account Registers (RPTARs) and come up with the certified list of taxpayers as basis for the setting up of RPT and SET Receivables, and (b) the Municipal Accountant follow the prescribed pro-forma accounting entries for the setting-up of RPT/SEF Receivables, and receipt of payment and distribution of RPT income in accordance with Section 36, Volume I of the NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The municipality used as basis, the Quarterly Report on RPT Assessment as of December 31, 2013 for the set-up of RPT/SET Receivable for CY 2014, only 80% of the total collectibles for both RPT and SET was taken up as receivables.
observations.reason: Incomplete record of taxpayers for the preparation of list of taxpayers with amount due and collectible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with the necessary documentary requirement for claims against government funds.
observations.managementAction: Some of the supporting documents were already submitted as of December 2013.
observations.reason: Some of the concerned offices did not submit the supporting documents as required.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Accountant ensure that the claims against government funds are supported with complete supporting documents in compliance with the provision of Section 4(6) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant submitted the supporting documents to some claims.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer breakdown/segregate the lump sum appropriations for different municipal programs/activities for the ensuing year into proper expense items based on prior years’ expenditures of the same programs/activities. And the Municipal Accountant classify the actual expenses to appropriate expense accounts so as to limit the expenses charged against the Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses account and draw an adjusting journal entry to take up the receivable which was erroneously recorded as expense of P60,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Starting mid of 2013, the use of Other MOOE account was only for transactions that cannot be booked or classified to any account code under the NGAS Manual. The Municipal Accountant classify the actual expenses to appropriate expense accounts so as to limit the expenses charged against the OMOOE and draw an adjusting journal entry to take up the receivable which was erroneously recorded as expense of P60,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accountant ensure that the ensuing claims against government funds are supported with complete supporting documents in compliance with the provision of Section 4(6) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Management made partial submission of the documents to support transactions with incomplete documentations totaling P291,572.89 and for other claims.
observations.reason: Still gathering for the supporting documents on other claims.
